title: How I Use AI To Code Faster (Without Losing Control of my Codebase)
author: Anubhav
text:
Member-only story
How I Use AI To Code Faster (Without Losing Control of my Codebase)
Practical guide to balancing speed and control when coding with LLMs
AI tools are everywhere. Feels like you can’t scroll through a tech blog without someone talking about their new AI-powered workflow. Until earlier this year, I just saw them as fancy autocomplete. Handy, but not something that would fundamentally change how I build stuff day-to-day as an engineer. But, I was a bit wrong.
Using AI effectively has become a real skill. It’s not just about typing a question and getting code back. It’s about knowing how to ask, when to trust the output, and most importantly, when to tell it to stop and just do the thing yourself. I’m going to walk you through the workflow I’ve built for myself. It’s what helps me get things done without losing my mind or letting the AI drive my codebase into a hole. This is what works for me, a 23-year-old engineer juggling ML, backend, and DevOps and maybe it’ll help you too.
My AI toolbox: more than one is key
My setup isn’t just one tool and I think it’s a common mistake most developers make. Different models are good at different things, like having a toolkit with more than just a hammer.
My primary setup is Cursor, with Claude 4.5 Sonnet as the default model and gemini-2.5-pro in between on slightly easier tasks. Cursor is great because it lives right in my editor. It sees my files, it…
